channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Has social justice become the new religion of the West?
item.description: Host Mohamed Hassan with guests asks: as religion fades in the West, what replaces it?

item.title: What Modi’s big win in Indian state elections could mean for its democracy
item.description: Modi's BJP won the key state of West Bengal for the first time. But the vote has thrown up questions that won't go away.

item.title: Can central banks curb inflation as energy costs rise?
item.description: Central banks hold rates steady as energy shock tests inflation fight.

item.title: Bayern Munich vs PSG: Champions League – preview, team news, start, lineups
item.description: PSG travel to Munich defending a 5-4 lead from the semifinal first leg, as both coaches promise more attacking football.

item.title: Tuareg rebels hold dozens of soldiers in Mali as prisoners
item.description: Exclusive video obtained by Al Jazeera shows dozens of soldiers held prisoner by Tuareg separatists in northern Mali.

item.title: ‘Starmer’s referendum’: How local elections could expose a fractured UK
item.description: Thursday’s polls could deliver a sharp setback for Labour, with Reform UK and the Greens expected to gain, analysts say.

item.title: Two hantavirus cases confirmed, five more suspected on stranded cruise ship
item.description: Among the seven cases, three people have died, one is critically ill and three have mild symptoms, the WHO says.

item.title: Taiwan player victim of ‘inappropriate contact’ at UK table tennis event
item.description: Officials say a female player was subjected to inappropriate physical contact during a security check.

item.title: US Secret Service shoots gunman near White House
item.description: The suspect has been taken to hospital following the shooting in Washington.

item.title: Macron sings as Armenia’s leader drums in unusual moment at state dinner
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron sang ‘La Boheme’ accompanied by Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on the drums.

item.title: Russian air attacks kill five at Ukraine’s Naftogaz gas facilities
item.description: Three workers and two emergency service rescuers ​killed and 37 others wounded, Naftogaz CEO Serhiy ​Koretskyi says.

item.title: Taiwan has ‘right to engage with the world’ after Eswatini visit
item.description: Taiwan has ‘right to engage with the world’ after Eswatini visit

item.title: NBA semifinal series: Timberwolves stun Spurs in Game 1; Knicks beat 76ers
item.description: Anthony Edwards returned from injury to score 18 points as the visitors beat San Antonio Spurs 104-102.

item.title: What Iran is saying about attack on UAE
item.description: What Iran is saying about attack on UAE

item.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of killing three civilians in ‘war crime’
item.description: The incident is the latest test of a fragile ceasefire agreed between the two countries last month.

item.title: What to know ahead of the Ohio primary election
item.description: Several races being closely watched as both parties prepare for the crucial midterm elections in November.

item.title: As summer approaches, skin diseases spread through Gaza’s refugee camps
item.description: Skin conditions have become a serious issue for healthcare workers in Gaza, particularly when temperatures rise.

item.title: In Mauritania, push to phase out private schools divides opinion
item.description: Officials say measure will ease systemic discrimination, but private school sector fears education quality is at risk.

item.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Israeli security agencies sound alarm on settlers
item.description: Israeli officials warn that violence threatens state, but attacks, land seizures and blockades continue in West Bank.

item.title: No FIFA World Cup 2026 broadcast deals signed in India, China
item.description: Discussions on the sale of media rights in both countries are ongoing, FIFA says weeks before the World Cup begins.

item.title: Nine workers killed in Colombia coal mine explosion
item.description: The national mining ​agency had warned weeks earlier that a buildup of gases at the mine could become dangerous.

item.title: Thiago Ávila’s letter from Israeli prison to his daughter
item.description: The Brazilian activist explains to his little girl his motivation to join the Gaza-bound humanitarian flotilla.

item.title: Explosion at fireworks factory in China kills 26, injures 61
item.description: Rescue efforts are still under way after a huge explosion ripped through China's fireworks capital.

item.title: ‘What comes next?’: Fear grips Pakistani families of Somali piracy victims
item.description: As piracy resurges off Somalia, families of hijacked tanker crew in Pakistan search for answers and help.

item.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 67 as Hormuz crisis deepens?
item.description: The UAE says it intercepted Iranian missiles after a suspected drone attack, with no comment from Iran.
